page.content: The table presents data across two headers: "Experience group number" and "Treatment level," with subsequent columns detailing "NaCl (g/L)" and "NaHCO3 (mM)." The first row, labeled 0, shows values of 0 g/L NaCl and 0 mM NaHCO3. The second row, labeled 1, contains 5 g/L NaCl and 15 mM NaHCO3. In the third row, labeled 2, the values are 5 g/L NaCl and 30 mM NaHCO3. The fourth row, labeled 3, shows 5 g/L NaCl and 45 mM NaHCO3. The fifth row, labeled 4, presents 8 g/L NaCl and 15 mM NaHCO3.

page.content: The sixth row, labeled 5, lists 8 g/L NaCl and 30 mM NaHCO3. The seventh row, labeled 6, records 8 g/L NaCl and 45 mM NaHCO3. The eighth row, labeled 7, indicates 11 g/L NaCl and 15 mM NaHCO3. In the ninth row, labeled 8, the values are 11 g/L NaCl and 30 mM NaHCO3. Finally, the tenth row, labeled 9, presents 11 g/L NaCl and 45 mM NaHCO3. This comprehensive overview includes all experience groups from 0 to 9, detailing the corresponding treatment levels, with variations in NaCl concentration ranging from 0 g/L to 11 g/L and NaHCO3 concentrations spanning from 0 mM to 45 mM, thereby illustrating the structured experimental design and treatment levels employed in this analysis.
